epigraph.text:
Seek and you will find; knock and the door will be 
opened to you.

content.text:
One-step equations are the simplest to solve. As the name suggests, 
they require only one operation to isolate the variable and find the 
solution.

When a number is added to the variable, subtract that number from 
both sides to isolate the variable.

content.title: Solving $x + a = b$
content.content:
To solve an equation where a number is added to the variable:

1. Subtract the same number from both sides

2. Simplify to find the value of the variable

3. Check your answer in the original equation

content.title: Addition equations
content.solution:
$$
x + 7 &= 12 && \text{Original equation} \\
x + 7 - 7 &= 12 - 7 && \text{Subtract 7 from both sides} \\
x &= 5 && \text{Simplify}
$$

**Check:** $5 + 7 = 12$

content.text:
When a number is subtracted from the variable, add that number to 
both sides.

content.content:
Solving equations is like finding lost treasure. Jesus said, "The 
kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field" (Matthew 13:44). 
Through systematic searching—applying inverse operations—we uncover 
the hidden value of $x$.

content.text:
When the variable is multiplied by a number, divide both sides by 
that number.

content.title: Solving $ax = b$
content.content:
To solve an equation where the variable is multiplied by a number:

1. Divide both sides by the coefficient of the variable

2. Simplify to find the value of the variable

3. Check your answer

content.text:
When the variable is divided by a number, multiply both sides by 
that number.

content.title: Scripture
content.content:
In the feeding of the 5,000 (John 6:1-14), Jesus multiplied five 
loaves and two fish to feed the multitude. Division and multiplication 
are inverse operations—just as Jesus could multiply abundance from 
scarcity, mathematics allows us to reverse any operation to find 
what we seek.

marginNotes.text:
Your word is a lamp for my feet, a 
light on my path.

From everyone who has been given much, 
much will be demanded.

marginNotes.author: Bertrand Russell
marginNotes.text:
Mathematics takes us into the region of 
absolute necessity.

marginNotes.author: Emmy Noether
marginNotes.text:
My methods are really methods of working 
and thinking.
